paragraphs.para: La Conférence internationale de l'Instruction publique, Convoquée à Genève par, l'Organisation des Nations Unies pour l'Éducation, la Science et la Culture et le Bureau international d'Éducation, et s'y étant réunie le vingt-huit juin en sa onzième session, adopte le trente juin mil neuf cent quarante-huit la recommandation suivante :
paragraphs.think: Tags LAW_INTER (international legal framework for education) and O_G (general academic orientation) are selected because the paragraph describes an international conference adopting a recommendation, which aligns with legal frameworks and general education principles. Related paragraphs 2-21 are parts of the same recommendation document, with para 2 (conference details), 3 (educational principles), 9 (recommendation submission), and 11/20/21 (specific pedagogical guidelines) providing complementary/supporting content.
paragraphs.para_en: The International Conference on Public Instruction, convened in Geneva by the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization and the International Bureau of Education, and having met there on the twenty-eighth of June in its eleventh session, adopts on the thirtieth of June nineteen hundred and forty-eight the following recommendation:

paragraphs.para: La Conférence,
paragraphs.think: The target paragraph references 'The Conference,' which likely sets the context for education-related discussions. 'Education orientation' and 'Subject domain' are selected as tags because the subsequent paragraphs (3-21) focus on educational techniques, curriculum elements, and pedagogical methods. All related paragraphs provide specific details about education practices, making them complemental to the overarching conference theme.
paragraphs.para_en: The Conference,

paragraphs.para: Considérant l'importance des techniques de base comme instruments de culture et d'instruction et comme éléments essentiels de l'éducation fondamentale,
paragraphs.think: Tags CUR_CONTENT and PEDAG_FOUND are selected because the paragraph emphasizes the role of basic techniques in education (content) and pedagogical foundations. Related paragraphs discuss writing techniques (4), handwriting instruction (10), creative activities (12), hand flexibility tools (17), and hygiene (23), which all connect to the target's focus on foundational educational practices.
paragraphs.para_en: Considering the importance of basic techniques as instruments of culture and instruction and as essential elements of fundamental education,

paragraphs.para: Que l'écriture n'est pas seulement une technique d'enseignement, mais un moyen d'expression et un art dont le style personnel doit conserver un maximum d'harmonie et d'élégance,
paragraphs.think: Tags: 'F_ARTS' is selected because the paragraph emphasizes writing as an art form requiring harmony and elegance. Relations: Para 14 (complemental) discusses writing's functional integration with reading, which complements the artistic perspective. Para 18 (complemental) addresses personal handwriting development, aligning with the emphasis on personal style. Para 19 (supporting) reinforces writing as a means rather than an end, directly supporting the paragraph's assertion.
paragraphs.para_en: That writing is not only a teaching technique, but a means of expression and an art whose personal style must retain the highest degree of harmony and elegance,

paragraphs.para: Que d'autre part le rythme de la vie moderne exige une écriture de plus en plus rapide,
paragraphs.think: The paragraph discusses the necessity of rapid handwriting due to modern life's pace, aligning with 'Curriculum' (as it pertains to educational requirements) and 'Education orientation' (focusing on educational priorities). Related paragraphs 7 and 18 directly address speed and quality in handwriting, providing supporting evidence. Paragraphs 10 and 6 discuss broader educational methods and child-focused approaches, which complement the target's context.
paragraphs.para_en: That, on the other hand, the pace of modern life requires increasingly rapid handwriting,

paragraphs.para: Que les progrès de la psychologie appliquée à l'éducation et de la pédagogie expérimentale permettent de concevoir des méthodes toujours mieux adaptées aux possibilités de l'enfant,
paragraphs.think: Tags PEDAG_METHO and PEDAG_FOUND are selected because the paragraph discusses teaching methods (PEDAG_METHO) and pedagogical foundations (PEDAG_FOUND) related to child capabilities. Related paragraphs: 7 (supporting specific methods), 11 (supporting evidence-based methods), 12 (complementary developmental stages), 15 (supporting adapted writing techniques), 16 (modifying aesthetic considerations).
paragraphs.para_en: That advances in psychology applied to education and experimental pedagogy allow for the development of methods increasingly better suited to the child's capabilities,

paragraphs.para: Que le but à atteindre est de donner à chaque enfant l'écriture la meilleure qu'il est capable de tracer à une vitesse suffisante,
paragraphs.think: Tags F_LITE (literacy focus), POP_CHILD (targeting children), and POL_QUAL (quality outcomes) are selected. Relations: Para 8 (complemental) addresses writing system diversity relevant to handwriting; Para 9 (supporting) provides recommendations aligned with the goal; Para 10 (supporting) directly reinforces the need for handwriting improvement; Para 18 (complemental) specifies cursive training for quality/speed; Para 20 (modifying) addresses adolescent-specific challenges; Para 22 (supporting) emphasizes teacher training for effective implementation.
paragraphs.para_en: That the goal to be achieved is to provide each child with the best handwriting they are capable of producing at an adequate speed,

paragraphs.para: Tout en tenant compte de la diversité des langues et des systèmes d'écriture,
paragraphs.think: Tags POL_INCL (Inclusion and diversity) and F_LITE (Literacy and language) are selected because the paragraph emphasizes considering diversity in languages and writing systems, which directly relates to inclusion and literacy. For relations: Para 13 discusses age-appropriate writing instruction, which complements the target's focus on diverse writing systems. Para 18 and 21 address writing instruction and evaluation, aligning with the literacy theme. Confidence scores reflect the indirect but relevant connections.
paragraphs.para_en: While taking into account the diversity of languages and writing systems,

paragraphs.para: Soumet aux Ministères de l'Instruction publique des différents pays la recommandation suivante :
paragraphs.think: The target paragraph (para 9) introduces a policy recommendation to education ministries, making 'POL_OTH' (Policy theme | Other policy theme) the most relevant tag. All subsequent paragraphs (10-22) are specific recommendations listed in the same document, forming a complementary relationship as they elaborate on the policy direction outlined in para 9.
paragraphs.para_en: Submits the following recommendation to the Ministries of Public Education of the various countries:

paragraphs.para: 1. L'amélioration de l'enseignement de l'écriture doit constituer l'une des préoccupations constantes des autorités scolaires et des éducateurs ;
paragraphs.think: Tags PEDAG_OTHER and ACT_EDUC are selected because the paragraph focuses on pedagogical concerns (PEDAG_OTHER) and stakeholder roles (ACT_EDUC). Relations: Para 11 (supporting) aligns with teacher methodological discretion; Para 12 (complemental) adds developmental stages; Para 22 (supporting) emphasizes teacher training; Para 21 (complemental) introduces evaluation metrics.
paragraphs.para_en: 1. The improvement of handwriting instruction must constitute one of the constant concerns of educational authorities and educators;

paragraphs.para: 2. Tout en jouissant d'une large initiative, le maître doit pouvoir s'inspirer de méthodes mises au point selon les données les plus récentes des sciences de l'éducation;
paragraphs.think: Tags 'Teachers' and 'CUR_IMPL' are selected because the paragraph emphasizes teachers' discretion and reliance on educational science methods (curriculum implementation). Paragraph 15 (simplified characters for children) supports the target's focus on science-based teaching methods. Paragraph 22 (teacher training in rational handwriting instruction) directly aligns with the target's emphasis on educators using evidence-based approaches.
paragraphs.para_en: 2. While enjoying wide discretion, the teacher should be able to draw inspiration from methods developed according to the most recent findings in the educational sciences;

paragraphs.para: 3. Une initiation concrète, sensorielle et motrice, donnant à l'enfant de nombreuses possibilités d'activités créatrices, devrait précéder pendant une période suffisamment longue l'acquisition des caractères et de la technique proprement dite de l'écriture;
paragraphs.think: Tags CUR_IMPL and F_LITE are selected because the paragraph discusses curriculum implementation (methods for teaching writing) and literacy (writing/reading skills). Related paragraphs: 13 (supports timing of writing instruction), 14 (complements integration of reading/writing), 15 (supports simplified characters for initiation), 16 (complements evolving character design), 17 (supports sensory tools for motor skills), and 18 (complements progression to cursive script).
paragraphs.para_en: 3. A concrete, sensory, and motor initiation, offering the child numerous opportunities for creative activities, should precede for a sufficiently long period the acquisition of characters and the proper technique of writing;

paragraphs.para: 4. En conséquence, l'enseignement systématique de l'écriture ne devrait pas débuter avec des enfants trop jeunes ;
paragraphs.think: The target paragraph discusses the age-appropriateness of starting systematic writing instruction (POP_CHILD: Learner population | Children (0-17)), which directly relates to the learner population tag. The Curriculum tag is relevant as it addresses the structure and timing of educational content. For relations: Para 14 (simultaneous reading/writing) and Para 18 (cursive script progression) complement the discussion of writing instruction stages. Para 20 (handwriting crisis in adolescence) provides related developmental context, all with moderate confidence as they address different aspects of writing education.
paragraphs.para_en: 4. Consequently, the systematic teaching of writing should not begin with children who are too young;

paragraphs.para: 5. L'apprentissage de l'écriture devrait avoir lieu simultanément avec celui de la lecture, afin qu'il ait un caractère vivant et fonctionnel ;
paragraphs.think: The paragraph discusses integrating writing and reading as foundational literacy skills (F_LITE) and addresses teaching methodologies (Pedagogy & assessment). It directly supports para 19's emphasis on writing as a functional tool rather than an end in itself, aligning their shared focus on practical literacy development.
paragraphs.para_en: 5. The learning of writing should take place simultaneously with that of reading, so that it has a lively and functional character;

paragraphs.para: 6. Des caractères aux formes simplifiées, adaptés aux modes de perception et d'assimilation de l'enfant, devraient servir de point de départ à une écriture claire, sobre et harmonieuse;
paragraphs.think: Tags 'M_FORM' (Learning modality) and 'Pedagogy & assessment' are selected because the paragraph discusses simplified characters adapted to children's perception (learning modality) and clear writing methods (pedagogy). Related paragraphs address complementary aspects: 16 (aesthetic evolution of characters), 17 (tools for hand flexibility), 22 (teacher training), 21 (evaluation scales), and 20 (corrective instruction). Relations are based on thematic alignment and progression in writing education.
paragraphs.para_en: 6. Characters with simplified shapes, adapted to the modes of perception and assimilation of the child, should serve as a starting point for clear, simple, and harmonious writing;

paragraphs.para: 7. Il est à souhaiter que la forme des caractères proposés aux enfants évolue suivant la conception esthétique de l'époque;
paragraphs.think: Tags 'CURRICULUM' and 'POP_CHILD' are selected because the paragraph discusses evolving writing instruction (curriculum) for children (POP_CHILD). Relations: Para 18 (supporting) aligns with writing instruction methods; Para 22 (supporting) addresses teacher training for handwriting; Para 20 (complemental) addresses adolescent handwriting challenges; Para 17 (complemental) provides tools for hand development.
paragraphs.para_en: 7. It is desirable that the form of the characters presented to children evolves according to the aesthetic conception of the time;

paragraphs.para: 8. Il convient au début d'utiliser des instruments favorisant la souplesse de la main: pinceau tendre, crayon tendre, plumes émoussées ;
paragraphs.think: The target paragraph focuses on initial writing tools (soft brushes, pencils) for hand flexibility, which falls under Arts and Culture (F_ARTS). Paragraph 18 (cursive script training) is a sequential step in writing instruction, making it complemental. Paragraph 22 (teacher training) supports the need for structured methods, hence supporting. Paragraph 23 (hygiene in teaching) adds environmental context, making it complemental.
paragraphs.para_en: At the beginning, it is appropriate to use instruments that promote hand flexibility: soft brush, soft pencil, blunt quills;

paragraphs.para: 9. Une fois les éléments de l'écriture acquis, il faut prévoir, tout en donnant à chacun la possibilité de développer une écriture individuelle, un entrainement à une écriture courante conciliant à la fois la qualité et la rapidité;
paragraphs.think: Tags CUR_DVPMT (Curriculum Design and Development) and F_LITE (Literacy and language) are selected because the paragraph discusses curriculum elements for handwriting training (CUR_DVPMT) and literacy skills (F_LITE). Relations: Para 19 (supporting) aligns with the purpose of writing as a refined skill; Para 20 (complemental) adds age-specific individualized instruction context; Para 22 (supporting) reinforces pedagogical training requirements.
paragraphs.para_en: Once the elements of writing have been acquired, it is necessary to provide, while allowing each individual the possibility to develop a personal handwriting, training in a cursive script that reconciles both quality and speed.

paragraphs.para: 10. L'écriture ne doit pas être considérée comme une fin en soi, mais comme un moyen qui doit être perfectionné à l'occasion de tous les travaux écrits;
paragraphs.think: The target paragraph emphasizes writing as a means for refinement (pedagogical approach), aligning with 'PEDAG_OTHER' (B2). Relations: Para 20 (complemental) addresses a specific refinement context; Para 21 (supporting) provides evaluation methods; Para 22 (supporting) reinforces teacher training; Para 23 (complemental) adds environmental factors.
paragraphs.para_en: 10. Writing should not be regarded as an end in itself, but as a means that must be refined through all written work;

paragraphs.para: 11. Il est désirable que soit donné entre 12 et 15 ans, moment où se produit une crise de l'écriture chez les adolescents, un enseignement correctif aussi individualisé que possible;
paragraphs.think: Tags POP_YOUTH (adolescents mentioned) and F_LITE (handwriting as literacy) are directly supported. Para 21 (evaluation scales) complements the corrective instruction focus. Para 22 (teacher training) supports the need for effective instruction methods.
paragraphs.para_en: It is desirable that, between the ages of 12 and 15, when a handwriting crisis occurs among adolescents, a corrective instruction be provided that is as individualized as possible.

paragraphs.para: 12. L'établissement d'échelles objectives d'évaluation de l'écriture est souhaitable ;
paragraphs.think: The paragraph discusses establishing objective evaluation scales for handwriting, which directly relates to pedagogy and assessment practices. Para 22 supports this by emphasizing teacher training in handwriting instruction, which aligns with implementing objective evaluation methods. The relation is strong as both address structured approaches to handwriting education.
paragraphs.para_en: 12. The establishment of objective scales for the evaluation of handwriting is desirable;

paragraphs.para: 13. Les maitres devraient être préparés à un enseignement rationnel de l'écriture et avoir eux-mêmes une écriture nette et lisible ;
paragraphs.think: The paragraph discusses teacher training in handwriting instruction (T_INI: Initial education and training). The related paragraph (23) addresses classroom hygiene and equipment, which complements the teaching environment context but does not directly overlap with the specific topic of handwriting training. The complemental relation reflects shared educational context with moderate confidence.
paragraphs.para_en: 13. Teachers should be trained in a rational approach to teaching handwriting and should themselves have clear and legible handwriting.

paragraphs.para: 14. L'hygiène jouant un rôle important dans l'enseignement de l'écriture, il faudrait accorder un soin particulier au choix du mobilier et du matériel ainsi qu'à l'éclairage des classes.
paragraphs.think: The paragraph focuses on pedagogical practices related to handwriting instruction, specifically environmental factors (furniture, equipment, lighting). The 'PEDAG_OTHER' tag is appropriate as it covers other pedagogy-related issues not explicitly categorized under the other tags. No related paragraphs exist in the allowed_paragraph_ids list.
paragraphs.para_en: 14. As hygiene plays an important role in the teaching of handwriting, particular care should be given to the selection of furniture and equipment as well as to the lighting of classrooms.

body.body_raw: La Conférence internationale de l'Instruction publique, Convoquée à Genève par, l'Organisation des Nations Unies pour l'Éducation, la Science et la Culture et le Bureau international d'Éducation, et s'y étant réunie le vingt-huit juin en sa onzième session, adopte le trente juin mil neuf cent quarante-huit la recommandation suivante : La Conférence, Considérant l'importance des techniques de base comme instruments de culture et d'instruction et comme éléments essentiels de l'éducation fondamentale, Que l'écriture n'est pas seulement une technique d'enseignement, mais un moyen d'expression et un art dont le style personnel doit conserver un maximum d'harmonie et d'élégance, Que d'autre part le rythme de la vie moderne exige une écriture de plus en plus rapide, Que les progrès de la psychologie appliquée à l'éducation et de la pédagogie expérimentale permettent de concevoir des méthodes toujours mieux adaptées aux possibilités de l'enfant, Que le but à atteindre est de donner à chaque enfant l'écriture la meilleure qu'il est capable de tracer à une vitesse suffisante, Tout en tenant compte de la diversité des langues et des systèmes d'écriture, Soumet aux Ministères de l'Instruction publique des différents pays la recommandation suivante : 1. L'amélioration de l'enseignement de l'écriture doit constituer l'une des préoccupations constantes des autorités scolaires et des éducateurs ; 2. Tout en jouissant d'une large initiative, le maître doit pouvoir s'inspirer de méthodes mises au point selon les données les plus récentes des sciences de l'éducation;  103 3. Une initiation concrète, sensorielle et motrice, donnant à l'enfant de nombreuses possibilités d'activités créatrices, devrait précéder pendant une période suffisamment longue l'acquisition des caractères et de la technique proprement dite de l'écriture; 4. En conséquence, l'enseignement systématique de l'écriture ne devrait pas débuter avec des enfants trop jeunes ; 5. L'apprentissage de l'écriture devrait avoir lieu simultanément avec celui de la lecture, afin qu'il ait un caractère vivant et fonctionnel ; 6. Des caractères aux formes simplifiées, adaptés aux modes de perception et d'assimilation de l'enfant, devraient servir de point de départ à une écriture claire, sobre et harmonieuse; 7. Il est à souhaiter que la forme des caractères proposés aux enfants évolue suivant la conception esthétique de l'époque; 8. Il convient au début d'utiliser des instruments favorisant la souplesse de la main: pinceau tendre, crayon tendre, plumes émoussées ; 9. Une fois les éléments de l'écriture acquis, il faut prévoir, tout en donnant à chacun la possibilité de développer une écriture individuelle, un entrainement à une écriture courante conciliant à la fois la qualité et la rapidité; 10. L'écriture ne doit pas être considérée comme une fin en soi, mais comme un moyen qui doit être perfectionné à l'occasion de tous les travaux écrits; 11. Il est désirable que soit donné entre 12 et 15 ans, moment où se produit une crise de l'écriture chez les adolescents, un enseignement correctif aussi individualisé que possible; 12. L'établissement d'échelles objectives d'évaluation de l'écriture est souhaitable ; 13. Les maitres devraient être préparés à un enseignement rationnel de l'écriture et avoir eux-mêmes une écriture nette et lisible ; 14. L'hygiène jouant un rôle important dans l'enseignement de l'écriture, il faudrait accorder un soin particulier au choix du mobilier et du matériel ainsi qu'à l'éclairage des classes.  104 —
